subject: ap-chemistry
title: Chemical Equilibrium
stimulus:
7. Strontium hydroxide dissolves in water according to the following equation. The K expression for strontium
sp
hydroxide is provided.
$Sr(OH)_2$(s)  Sr 2+ ( aq) + 2OH − (aq) K = [Sr 2+] [$OH^-$ ]2
2 sp

parts.prompt:
A student draws the particulate diagram shown to represent the ions present in an aqueous solution of
$Sr(OH)_2$ . (Water molecules are intentionally omitted.) Identify the error in the student’s drawing.

The student prepares a saturated solution by adding excess $Sr(OH)_2$(s)to distilled water and stirring until no
more solid dissolves. The student then determines that [Sr 2+] = 0.043 M in the solution.
(i) Calculate the value of [$OH^-$] in the solution.
(ii) Calculate the value of K for $Sr(OH)_2$ .
sp 2
rubric_criteria: (i) For the correct calculated value: 1 point
(ii) For the correct calculated value, consistent with (b)(i): 1 point

parts.prompt:
The student prepares a second saturated solution of Sr( OH) in aqueous 0.10 M Sr(NO ) instead of water.
2 3 2
Will the value of [$OH^-$] in the second solution be greater than, less than, or equal to the value in the first
solution? Justify your answer. (Assume constant temperature.)
rubric_criteria: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
